2026-04-17T16:34:30.6084049Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T16:34:30.6084568Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T16:34:30.6084977Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T16:34:30.6085332Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T16:34:30.6085621Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T16:34:30.6085852Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T16:34:30.6086012Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-17T16:34:30.6086597Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-17T16:34:30.6087076Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T16:34:30.6087325Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-17T16:37:37.8705890Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T16:35:35.438769806Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58299,\"title\":\"[ResourceGraph] Docs: Initial README gaps in Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph 1.0.0-beta.1\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (post-release version bump for PR #58257) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nInitial public release (1.0.0-beta.1) of `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph`, which enables declarative provisioning of Azure Resource Graph shared queries using the Azure Provisioning library pattern. The package exposes the `ResourceGraphQuery` resource type backed by `Microsoft.ResourceGraph/queries@2024-04-01`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section**: The `### Authenticate the Client` sub-heading under \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; exists but contains zero content. Consumers need guidance on what credentials to supply when using `azd` or the Azure Provisioning infrastructure APIs.\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section**: The `## Next steps` heading exists but has no content — no links to API reference, related provisioning packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning.Resources`), or the `azd` deployment workflow.\\n- **`FromExisting` pattern is undocumented**: `ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(...)` allows referencing a pre-existing Resource Graph query in a larger infrastructure definition, which is a key Provisioning library pattern. This method is not mentioned anywhere in the README.\\n- **Output properties not explained**: The read-only output properties `ResultKind`, `ModifiedOn`, and `Id` can be referenced in downstream Bicep expressions. The README has no mention of reading outputs or using them in multi-resource configurations.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n1. Fill in the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section (after the `### Prerequisites` section):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` is a declarative provisioning library — it generates Bicep/ARM templates rather than calling Azure APIs directly at runtime. Authentication is handled by the deployment tooling:\\n\\n- When using **Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)**, run `azd auth login` to authenticate before deploying.\\n- When deploying programmatically, provide an `ArmClient` with your preferred [`TokenCredential`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) (e.g., `DefaultAzureCredential`).\\n\\nFor more information on authentication, see the [Azure Identity README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md).\\n```\\n\\n2. Add a second example under `## Examples` for the `FromExisting` pattern (after the existing query-creation example):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Reference an Existing Resource Graph Query\\n\\nUse `FromExisting` to reference a shared query that is already deployed, so you can wire its outputs into other resources:\\n\\n```\\nResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery));\\nexistingQuery.Name = \\u0026#34;my-existing-query\\u0026#34;;\\ninfra.Add(existingQuery);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n3. Fill in the `## Next steps` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- [Azure Resource Graph documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [API reference for Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure Provisioning library overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\\n- [Deploy infrastructure with Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure.Provisioning.Resources — use ResourceGroup, Subscription, and other ARM primitives alongside your query](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning.Resources/README.md)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Snippet `ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting`**\\n\\nWrite the sample code in a new test file at:\\n`sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/Samples/SampleFromExisting.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. The package exposes the `ResourceGraphQuery` resource type backed by `Microsoft.ResourceGraph/queries@2024-04-01`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section**: The `### Authenticate the Client` sub-heading under \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; exists but contains zero content. Consumers need guidance on what credentials to supply when using `azd` or the Azure Provisioning infrastructure APIs.\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section**: The `## Next steps` heading exists but has no content — no links to API reference, related provisioning packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning.Resources`), or the `azd` deployment workflow.\\n- **`FromExisting` pattern is undocumented**: `ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(...)` allows referencing a pre-existing Resource Graph query in a larger infrastructure definition, which is a key Provisioning library pattern. This method is not mentioned anywhere in the README.\\n- **Output properties not explained**: The read-only output properties `ResultKind`, `ModifiedOn`, and `Id` can be referenced in downstream Bicep expressions. The README has no mention of reading outputs or using them in multi-resource configurations.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n1. Authentication is handled by the deployment tooling:\\n\\n- When using **Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)**, run `azd auth login` to authenticate before deploying.\\n- When deploying programmatically, provide an `ArmClient` with your preferred [`TokenCredential`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) (e.g., `DefaultAzureCredential`).\\n\\nFor more information on authentication, see the [Azure Identity README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md).\\n```\\n\\n2.
